target.title: feat: add Hyper-V provider for local Windows VMs
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a Hyper-V SSH lease provider for running Crabbox workloads on local Windows VMs.

- Generation 2 VMs from pre-configured VHDX templates (ISO not supported)
- SSH key injection via PowerShell Direct (`Invoke-Command -VMName`) with retry+backoff
- Guest password passed via environment variable, not command arguments
- VHDX template copied per lease; only provider-created boot disk cleaned on release
- Shared native-Windows readiness check (git + tar + work root)
- `PublicNet.IPv4.IP` populated from live query and claim fallback
- Provider aliases: `local-hyperv`, `hyper-v`, `windows-vm`

## Files changed

| File | What |
| --- | --- |
| `internal/providers/hyperv/backend.go` | Provider implementation: Acquire, Resolve, List, Release, Cleanup, Touch |
| `internal/providers/hyperv/provider.go` | Provider registration and Configure |
| `internal/providers/hyperv/core.go` | Types, helpers, PowerShell wrappers |
| `internal/providers/hyperv/flags.go` | CLI flag registration |
| `internal/providers/hyperv/provider_test.go` | 32 unit tests |
| `internal/cli/config.go` | HyperVConfig struct, file+env loading |
| `internal/providers/all/all.go` | Side-effect import |
| `docs/providers/hyperv.md` | Full provider documentation |
| `docs/providers/README.md` | Provider index entry |
| `docs/source-map.md` | Source map entry |
| `internal/cli/providers_builtin_test.go` | Integration test coverage |

## What is NOT covered

- **No live Hyper-V VM proof yet.** The Hyper-V PowerShell module is enabled but not loadable on the dev host (known Windows 11 module directory desync). Live proof will follow once the module is repaired or tested on another host.
- WSL2 mode is explicitly rejected (native Windows only).

## Test proof

All 32 provider unit tests pass on Windows (`go test ./internal/providers/hyperv/... -v`):


## ClawSweeper local review

7 rounds of local ClawSweeper review. Progression: patchTier F -> D -> C -> C -> C -> B -> B. All code findings resolved:

1. VHDX template copy instead of blank VHD creation
2. SSH key injection via PowerShell Direct
3. Guest password moved from command args to env var (`_CRABBOX_GP`)
4. ISO support removed (VHDX-only)
5. `resolveInstance` propagates errors (no synthetic State=2)
6. VHD cleanup scoped to provider-created boot disk only
7. Post-boot key injection retry with backoff
8. VHDX orphan cleanup on `New-VM` failure
9. Shared Windows readiness check (git + tar + work root)
10. `PublicNet.IPv4.IP` populated for status/list/warmup views
11. `applyDefaults` forces TargetOS=windows unconditionally

Security review: cleared (password via env, VM removal scoped to crabbox- prefix).
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
